title: Clean up a Bash harness background process group
prompt: Fix `bin/run_case.sh`, whose background-job lifecycle tracks only the immediate child PID. Each command must run in its own pinned process group while retaining the harness stdin it needs during the run. EXIT, HUP, INT, and TERM handling must clean up the entire group with bounded graceful termination and a forced fallback, reap the tracked child, and leave no live orphan descendants that can consume later test input. Preserve the command's exact exit status on ordinary completion and return conventional signal statuses (129 for HUP, 130 for INT, and 143 for TERM). Keep cleanup diagnostics quiet. The offline verifier does not provide a usable `/dev/null`, so the harness must not rely on that device. Do not modify the protected tests. Run `python3 -B tests/test_background_cleanup.py` from the repository root; verification must remain offline and deterministic.
